what_it_does: This file provides a render-hint layer for decision records: a HINT map, a shape selector, and two pure helpers that produce an HTML/SVG infographic device and a slide-content object. It maps each decision to a calm, data-driven visual shape (duo, hero-split, checkMatrix, taxonomy, bars, ledger) without altering the authored decision text.
observations: A HINT registry keyed by decision id stores device choice, diagram seed data, shapeLine, and optional figure/figureLabel.
decisionShape prefers record.shape, then the HINT map, then a structural heuristic based on option count and recommended label.
decisionDevice dispatches to DNA.org renderers (hubNetwork, checkMatrix, bars, connector, taxonomyEmergence) and returns empty strings if a renderer is absent.
decisionSlide builds a slide object from record.legibility, the HINT shapeLine, the options list, and a reading-protocol points array.
The file exposes DNA._decisionHints for external authoring/harness use.
Comments frame the file as a strict lane boundary: render hints only, no authored meaning, no dashboard furniture.
The duo device wraps plates and a reversible axis in a vertical stack to avoid a noted overlap bug.
A policy is stated that figure/figureLabel should only be emitted when they represent a real domain quantity.
standouts: The registry-is-truth shape selector with graceful structural fallback.
The separation of card visual zone (device) from slide content (full option implications).
The data-driven dispatch to named DNA.org renderers rather than per-card hand-built markup.
The explicit design rule that visual devices must lift craft and stay calmer than a dashboard.
conventions: Uses an IIFE to attach helpers to a global DNA namespace.
Uses optional DNA.org renderer lookup with silent empty-string fallback.
Reads authored fields such as record.shape, record.legibility.name, record.legibility.why, and record.options.
Uses a switch dispatch over a finite device vocabulary.
Comments embed design rationale and revision notes (R2/R3) directly in the source.
concerns: The structural heuristic regex /^(both|a mix)\b/i is English-specific and fragile.
decisionDevice accepts an opt argument but does not use it.
The taxonomy device hardcodes provisional/real labels instead of drawing them from the HINT map or record.
The default fallback duo state uses 'Save-back on', which conflicts with the one-token invariant stated in the HINT comment.
Several device branches silently return empty strings if DNA.org renderers are missing, making failures invisible.
notes: A thin, opinionated render-hint layer that turns decision records into calm, shape-matched diagrams and standardized slide objects while leaving authored copy untouched.
questions: What is the exact schema and location of DNA.org and its renderer functions?
What contract do DNA.org renderers expect for arguments and return values?
How are the CSS classes referenced here (ar-duo, ar-bars, ar-matrix) styled?
What is the schema of content/decisions.json and how is record.shape authored?
What are DNA.renderArchetype and DNA.renderExplained, and how do they consume these outputs?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a render-hint layer that selects visual devices and derives slide content without touching authored meaning.
apparent_themes: Separation of render hints from authored recollection.
Calm, craft-led visualization over dashboard density.
Data-driven shape selection from option structure.
Card/slide content split to preserve legibility on small screens.
Reversibility as a first-class property of decisions.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to make every decision card visually distinctive and immediately readable by deriving the right diagram shape from the decision's own structure, while keeping the authored text in its own lane and preventing information overload.
novelty: A shape registry and heuristic that map decision option topology to specific calm infographic devices, combined with a deliberate policy of moving full option explanations into the slide rather than the card.
contribution: It contributes a centralized, extensible way to assign visual devices to decisions, a standardized slide-content builder, and an abstraction over concrete rendering implementations via DNA.org renderers.
summary_for_embedding: decision-render.js is a render-hint layer that maps decision records to calm, data-driven infographic devices and slide-content objects using a HINT registry, shape selector, and DNA.org renderer dispatch.
intention_for_embedding: This file was intended to give each decision a varied but mechanically derived visual shape and a clean slide presentation without hand-building cards or altering authored copy.

symbols.does: Returns the DNA.org namespace, which supplies the concrete SVG/HTML renderer functions used by decisionDevice.
symbols.description: A small accessor defined as `const org = () => DNA.org || {};`. It is called inside decisionDevice to look up optional renderer helpers such as hubNetwork, checkMatrix, bars, connector, and taxonomyEmergence. If DNA.org is absent, all device branches that depend on it return empty strings.

symbols.does: Selects a device name for a decision record using registry-first, then heuristic fallback.
symbols.description: This function returns record.shape if present, otherwise looks up the HINT map by record.id, and finally falls back to a structural heuristic based on option count and the recommended option label. It is the single source of truth for which visual device a decision wears.

symbols.does: Generates the HTML/SVG infographic string for a decision card's visual zone.
symbols.description: This function reads the selected shape and the HINT entry for the decision, then dispatches to the appropriate DNA.org renderer to produce a calm diagram. It handles hero-split, checkMatrix, bars, duo, taxonomy, and a default empty ledger case. It returns an empty string when a required renderer is unavailable.

symbols.does: Builds a slide-content object that presents the decision's full meaning and reading protocol.
symbols.description: This function combines archetype metadata, record.legibility fields, the HINT shapeLine, the options list, and a small reading-protocol points list into a structured object intended for DNA.renderExplained. It deliberately moves full option implications into the slide so the card can stay compact.
